decisions.rationale: Distinct SMC liquidity-sweep setup (Turtle Soup) using FVG rejection and swing-low raiding. No overlap with other candidates in this queue.

Intraday compression breakout setup using 9/21 EMA support with VWAP as the target level — distinct from the VWAP-first entries in cand_20064544cea6 and cand_432e171d0575 because trigger is EMA-based, not VWAP-based.
Long-only paired-ETF allocation model (TQQQ/TMF/IEF) with a crash-triggered rotation. Not a trade-entry pattern or price-action setup; no overlap with any pattern-based candidate.

VWAP-deviation mean-reversion entry targeting VWAP reversion. Distinct from cand_432e171d0575 and cand_74a3cf2a8bd7 because it is mean-reversion (not breakout/breakback) and uses VWAP deviation bands as entry, not as a breakout level.

VWAP breakback entry after a pre-market spike pullback, with partial-exit scaling. Distinct from cand_20064544cea6 (mean reversion to VWAP) and cand_9aed657c3a20 (10-sec chart + MACD filter) — the scaling rules and partial-exit mechanics are unique.
200-SMA dip-buy with per-tranche profit target. Distinct approach using a broad-market moving-average threshold, independent tranche accounting, and no stop loss. No overlap with any other candidate.
Darvas Box breakout setup — multi-week consolidation range breakout with sector filter. Distinct from the intraday/high-frequency patterns in the queue; no overlap with the EMA or VWAP candidates.

VWAP breakout with MACD-positive confirmation on a 10-second chart — ultra-short timeframe and dual-filter entry distinguish it from cand_432e171d0575 (scaling-based exit) and cand_20064544cea6 (mean reversion, not breakout).
